Servitors are parts of minds that are used by [[Sapients]] and [[asynthesizers]] in general by [[Learners]].

Servitors are mental machines that take inputs and give outputs. They are often linked to specific skills such as reading or writing. 

Servitors may be called "models" colloquially, but they are incapable of being transmitted linguistically, although [[brain uploading]] may facilitate the cloning of a servitor from a [[sapient]] or [[Asynthesizers|asynthesizer]].

One model of [[Sapients]] is that their entire mind is a single servitor that recursively produces other servitors for tasks. This model can be practically useful, but it fails to take into account that [[desire synthesis]] is often actively performed even in sapients, with infant [[humans]] being effectively identical to [[asynthesizers]] before their [[desire synthesis]] process 

== See Also ==

* [[Procedural knowing]]

[[Category:Sapient Psychology]]
[[sn:Servitors]][[sn:Servitors]][[Category:Learner Psychology]]
[[Category:Git synced pages]]
[[sn:Servitors]][[Category:Servitors]]
[[sn:Servitors]]
[[Category:Pages]]
